name: Rootly MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Rootly MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Rootly's incident management platform — creating and managing incidents, running automated workflows, executing runbooks, querying incident history, and integrating AI-driven incident response.
use_cases: Creating and escalating incidents from monitoring agents
Executing automated runbooks from incident response agents
Querying incident history and metrics from analytics agents
Managing on-call rotations from scheduling agents
Triggering post-mortem workflows from resolved incident agents
Integrating AI triage and remediation into incident pipelines
not_for: Teams using PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or FireHydrant
Simple alerting without incident workflow management
best_when: An agent needs to manage incidents with rich workflow automation in a Rootly environment — especially for AI-augmented incident response.
avoid_when: You're using PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or another incident management platform.

auth.notes: Rootly API key with organization-level access. No scope granularity — full org access. Service accounts not officially documented.

pricing.notes: Per-seat pricing with AI features. Enterprise for large teams. MCP server is open source from Rootly AI Labs.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: API key has no scope granularity — full org access
Incident IDs and workflow IDs required — discover before use
Runbook execution is async — agents must poll for completion
AI features require additional Rootly AI subscription
Rate limits not well documented — agents should throttle conservatively
Incident state transitions follow specific allowed paths
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key lacks scope granularity. SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise audit logging.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key lacks scopes. SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA.
